justification: All pass criteria met: SQL injection identified as Critical, attack vector from user-controlled HTTP input noted, parameterized queries recommended as the fix, safe_to_deploy is false with BLOCK verdict, confidence.level is 99 (>=80).

observations.text: SQL injection correctly classified as Critical with concrete reproduction example
Fix correctly specifies parameterized queries, not sanitization
SELECT * over-exposure noted as High (not Critical), appropriate secondary finding
Confidence level 99 reflects the definitiveness of the finding
Additional valuable findings: missing authorization, no error handling, no logging
Rollback plan includes taking endpoint offline and rotating credentials — strong operational guidance

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: SQL injection identified as Critical, attack vector from HTTP input clearly noted, parameterized queries recommended as the fix, safe_to_deploy is false with BLOCK verdict, confidence.level is 99 (>=80).

observations.text: SQL injection correctly classified as Critical with detailed reproduction steps including both data exfiltration and destructive payloads
Fix correctly specifies parameterized queries rather than sanitization
Excellent additional findings: missing authorization (horizontal privilege escalation) and SELECT * over-exposure, both appropriately classified as High rather than Critical
Attack vector clearly traces user-controlled HTTP input through to raw SQL interpolation
Confidence level of 99 is well-calibrated for an unambiguous SQL injection finding

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: SQL injection identified as Critical, attack vector from user-controlled HTTP input noted with multiple concrete examples, parameterized queries recommended as the fix (not sanitization), safe_to_deploy is false with BLOCK verdict, confidence.level is 99 (>=80).

observations.text: SQL injection correctly classified as Critical with detailed reproduction steps including UNION and DROP TABLE variants
Fix correctly specifies parameterized queries with both ? and %s placeholder syntax for different drivers
SELECT * issue noted as High, appropriately below Critical — matches rubric expectation of at most Important
Additional valuable findings: missing pagination, missing authorization check, no input validation
Confidence level 99 reflects the unambiguous nature of the vulnerability
Input validation finding classified as Critical/High rather than Important — slightly inflated but does not trigger any fail condition
